generated.description: Detailed schematic of the codec-guided distillation objective (Hypothesis 1). Show: float teacher path producing logits and reference tensors; student path with fake-quantized weights/activations; computation of L_task and KL distillation loss from teacher logits; selection of specific quantized tensors (e.g., final-layer weights or activations), conversion to bytes, zlib.compress call, computation of compressed bits-per-byte; comparison against a teacher- or baseline-derived target alpha to form L_comp=(L_zlib-alpha)^2; and final combination L_total=L_task+lambda_kd*L_KD+lambda_comp*L_comp. Include a small inset showing sparse evaluation of L_comp every N steps on CPU.

generated.description: Visualization of compression-biased stochastic gating (Hypothesis 2). For a single representative layer, depict multiple channels/heads with associated EMA statistics h_c (zlib bpb per channel). Show periodic measurement: activations are quantized, split per channel, passed through zlib, yielding bpb_c and updating h_c. Then illustrate how p_drop(c) is computed from h_c (higher bpb -> higher drop probability) and used to sample binary gates g_c during training. Emphasize that at inference all gates are fixed to 1 (no stochasticity) and the graph matches the baseline.
